title: Design a dimensional model from the grain up
description: Define the fact grain, then dimensions and measures, choosing star or snowflake to fit query and maintenance needs.
body: Use when modeling a business process for analytics. Do not start from source tables — start from the question and the grain. Actions: declare the fact table's grain (one row per what), identify the dimensions and additive measures, build conformed dimensions shared across facts, and choose a denormalized star for query simplicity or a normalized snowflake when dimension reuse and storage justify it. Validation signals include a fact table with a single declared grain and conformed dimensions reused across marts. Risk boundary: a mixed-grain fact table produces double-counted measures; if rows do not share one grain, treat the model as broken and re-derive it before building reports.
